nation: Première Nation des Innus de Nutashkuan
economic_notes: Community has significant fisheries operations (crab sales $2,288,230), Pourvoirie Hipou outfitter ($392,265 revenue), forestry operations (Produits Forestiers Innus SEC 51%), construction (Construction Atik SEC via Placements Innus-Nutashkuan), Hydro-Quebec Romaine-2 impact agreement generating annual payments. 63% of revenue from SAC and Health Canada. COVID-19 pandemic noted as subsequent event.
notes: Financial statements are for fiscal year ended March 31, 2020, but were not audited and accepted until January 18, 2022 - a nearly 2-year delay. Two government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method: Placements Innus-Nutashkuan SEC (100% owned, holding company with subsidiaries Construction Atik SEC 51% and 9286-1020 forestry 51%) and Produits Forestiers Innus SEC (51% owned, inactive/negative equity). Litigation contingencies noted including $2,414,606 lawsuit against Construction Atik and $1,686,667 in receivables from Produits Forestiers Innus litigation. Prior period adjustment of $1,607,859 recorded directly to opening surplus. SAC funding reconciliation shows total SAC contributions of $20,592,852 per note 14. Total government transfers estimated from revenue lines including SAC, Health Canada, SAA, SCHL, CLPN, Ishkuteu, MPO, CSSSPNQL, Institut Tshakapesh, MTQ. COVID-19 noted as significant subsequent event. Hydro-Quebec Romaine-2 impact agreement is major economic driver with long-term debt secured against these revenues.
